content.problemStatement:
You’ve just started developing a new feature and want to work on it safely **without touching the main code**.

Your Git repository is already initialized and has an initial commit. \
Follow below steps and use the correct Git commands you’ve learned so far:

**Step 1:** \
Create a new branch named `feature-branch` for your feature.

**Step 2:** \
Check all available branches to confirm that `feature-branch` was successfully created.

**Step 3:** \
Move to your `feature-branch` so you can start making changes there.

**Step 4:** \
Verify that you’re currently on the `feature-branch`.

**Step 5:** \
Once you’re done exploring, go back to your `main branch`.
